reviews.author: David Hildenbrand
reviews.summary: Requested additional information about the performance implications of this change and how it will be used in practice.

2026-03-10.patch_summary: This patch adds a new tracepoint called contended_release, which fires on the holder side when a contended lock is released. This complements existing contention_begin and contention_end tracepoints that fire on the waiter side. The new tracepoint enables correlating lock hold time under contention with waiter events by lock address.

reviews.author: Usama Arif
reviews.summary: Reviewer Usama Arif noted that the patch's exception for percpu_up_write() could be simplified by using waitqueue_active(&sem->waiters) to check if there are blocked waiters, making it consistent with other call sites.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello!

I saw that you mentioned in the commmit message that you do this for only
blocked waiters except for percpu_up_write(). We can use
waitqueue_active(&sem->waiters) to check for this over here so that
its consistent with every other call?

2026-03-12.patch_summary: This patch adds a new tracepoint called contended_release, which fires when a lock is released under contention. The patch complements existing contention_begin and contention_end tracepoints by providing information from the holder's perspective. It enables correlating lock hold time under contention with waiter events by lock address. The patch introduces calls to trace_contended_release() in slowpath unlock paths of various sleepable locks, including mutex, rtmutex, semaphore, rwsem, percpu-rwsem, and RT-specific rwbase locks.

reviews.author: Dmitry Ilvokhin (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged a potential issue in the patch, specifically that using waitqueue_active() instead of wq_has_sleeper() might be incorrect because it requires a barrier and they are not holding the lock at this point. They agreed to make the correction.

Author acknowledged a concern about inconsistent tracepoint placement, moved it to fire after lock release but before rcuwait_wake_up(), and plans to make other call sites consistent in v3.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks for the feedback, Usama.

I thought about it and even mentioned in the comment, but I forgot what
was the reason. Now, I think you are correct. I added wq_has_sleeper()
locally instead of waitqueue_active() locally, since we are not holding
the lock here and waitqueue_active() requires a barrier based on the
comment. It might be not very important here, but I'd rather make it
correct even for tracepoint.

Note that __percpu_up_read() doesn't need this guard. Maybe I was
thinking at __percpu_up_read() part before and just made it symmetric.

Anyway, thanks for suggestion.

---

Good point. I moved it after this_cpu_dec() so the tracepoint fires
after the lock is released but before rcuwait_wake_up(). I also went
through all other call sites and made the placement consistent where
possible: after release, before wake. It should be fixed in v3.

2026-03-16.patch_summary: This patch adds a new tracepoint called contended_release, which fires when a lock is released under contention. The patch complements existing contention_begin and contention_end tracepoints by providing information from the holder's perspective. It enables correlating lock hold time under contention with waiter events by lock address. The patch introduces calls to trace_contended_release() in slowpath unlock paths of various sleepable locks, including mutex, rtmutex, semaphore, rwsem, percpu-rwsem, and RT-specific rwbase locks.
